privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a real VLT V-band FITS image of the globular cluster Messier 12 (m12_Vcomb.fits), but the reference catalog and target limiting magnitude are synthetically generated during task setup. The setup script extracts actual star centroids using a Gaussian filter but assigns synthetic V magnitudes using simple aperture photometry and an arbitrary zero point of 25.0 (v_mag = -2.5 * log10(flux) + 25.0). A ground truth limiting magnitude is then dynamically calculated by linearly fitting log10(SNR) to the synthetic V magnitudes and evaluating at SNR=5. The verifier parses the output file to check that at least 8 stars are measured, requires a physically consistent negative Spearman rank correlation (<= -0.4) between V magnitude and SNR, and confirms the reported limiting magnitude is within 1.0 of the synthetically derived ground truth.
